Intentionally not registered in settings.json (documented as a policy stub with no active validation logic). 2 commits, recently modified (0 days). No violation. Note: snake_case naming (pre_slashcommand_validate.sh) is a pre-existing legacy convention in hooks/ — the directory has mixed naming conventions (snake_case and kebab-case coexisting); no authoritative folder rule mandates pure kebab-case. Per Codex finding #4 a naming violation finding would be a false positive.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex identified 4 issues: (1) Initial draft included a deletion recommendation for deprecated stubs that violates Safety Rules — files modified <7 days and have live documentary references; (2) Deprecated stubs should be reclassified as obsolete_functionality with severity: major and blocks_current_diff: false; (3) README staleness findings should be anchored to the causal changed files (bash_context_strip.py / test_bash_safety_context.py) rather than the README files themselves; (4) A naming-convention finding for pre_slashcommand_validate.sh (snake_case) is a false positive given acknowledged mixed legacy conventions in the hooks/ directory.

findings.issue: Deletion recommendation for deprecated stubs violates Safety Rules — files were modified <7 days ago and have live documentary references in docs/guides and docs/examples
findings.proposed_fix: Change action to: keep no-op deprecated stub; do not recommend deletion. Future cleanup requires user-approved retirement of doc/example references first and must satisfy Safety Rules (>7 days since modification, no live references).
findings.rationale: Applied. Removed any deletion recommendation. Added explicit Safety Rules compliance check listing documentary references that must be retired first before deletion is safe.

findings.issue: Deprecated stubs severity should be major (obsolete_functionality), not minor; should include blocks_current_diff: false
findings.proposed_fix: Reclassify as category: obsolete_functionality, severity: major, introduced_in_diff: false, blocks_current_diff: false
findings.rationale: Applied. Moved findings to obsolete_functionality category with severity: major, introduced_in_diff: false, blocks_current_diff: false.

findings.issue: README staleness findings were attributed to the README files themselves; in --changed-files mode findings should be anchored to the causal changed files
findings.proposed_fix: Reattach findings to hooks/lib/bash_context_strip.py and hooks/tests/test_bash_safety_context.py respectively (the actual changed files whose addition caused the README staleness).
findings.rationale: Applied. Findings are now in diff_scoped_findings[] anchored to the causal changed files (bash_context_strip.py, test_bash_safety_context.py) with a note about the affected README.

findings.issue: pre_slashcommand_validate.sh naming (snake_case vs kebab-case) is a false positive — the hooks/ directory has acknowledged mixed legacy conventions and no single authoritative folder rule mandates pure kebab-case
findings.proposed_fix: Remove naming violation finding for pre_slashcommand_validate.sh entirely. If retained, downgrade to OBSERVATION_ONLY with introduced_in_diff: false.
findings.rationale: Applied. Finding removed from active findings. Rationale documented in no_findings_rationale section for pre_slashcommand_validate.sh.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 codex findings applied: (1) deletion recommendation removed for deprecated stubs; (2) reclassified as obsolete_functionality/major with blocks_current_diff: false; (3) README stale findings re-anchored to causal changed files in diff_scoped_findings[]; (4) naming convention false positive removed.
